examples.title: Concurrent TCP Echo Server with Zombie Prevention
examples.description: Full implementation of concurrent TCP server handling multiple clients
examples.explanation: This concurrent server uses fork() to handle each client in a separate process. The SIGCHLD handler reaps zombie processes. SO_REUSEADDR allows quick restart. Parent closes connfd after fork to prevent resource leak.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) process memory for n clients

examples.title: TCP Client Connection
examples.description: Client code to connect to TCP server
examples.explanation: The client creates a socket, sets up the server address structure using inet_pton() to convert the IP string, then calls connect() which initiates the TCP 3-way handshake. After connection, data can be read/written.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for connection, O(n) for data transfer

examples.title: UDP Server and Client
examples.explanation: UDP uses sendto()/recvfrom() for connectionless communication. No listen() or accept() is needed. Each sendto specifies the destination. connect() can optionally be called to set a default peer for UDP.
